fact: February 27 is exactly 58 days into the year, which means there are only 307 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when maple trees start getting ready to make syrup - their sap begins flowing faster as temperatures swing between freezing at night and warmer during the day.
February 27 falls smack in the middle of Pisces season, making it one of the most 'watery' days of the year according to astrology.
In Antarctica, this date marks the peak of summer - penguins are molting their old feathers and growing shiny new ones for winter.
Earthworms in temperate climates start wiggling closer to the surface around this time, preparing for their big spring emergence when the soil fully thaws.
The sun rises about 2 minutes earlier each day in late February, meaning February 27 has roughly 4 more minutes of daylight than February 25.
In Japan, plum blossoms (called 'ume') are typically bursting open right around February 27, creating pink and white flower explosions before cherry blossoms even think about blooming.
February 27 sits exactly 3 weeks before the spring equinox, making it the perfect 'almost spring but not quite' day of the year.
Bears in northern regions are starting to get restless in their dens around this date - mama bears with new cubs begin preparing for their first venture outside.
The moon phase on February 27 changes every year, but when it's a full moon on this date, it creates some of the brightest late-winter nights because snow reflects the moonlight like a giant mirror.
In the Southern Hemisphere, February 27 is when many spider species start spinning extra webs - they're preparing for autumn and need to catch more food before winter.
